qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint
description: Complete rewrite of OpenTabs documentation to be world-class: role-based progressive depth (user → plugin developer → platform contributor), clean information architecture, SVG illustrations matching RetroUI neo-brutalist theme, no noise, hand-holding friendly tone. Restructure navigation, header bar, sidebar sections, and all content pages.

userStories.title: Restructure navigation config with new information architecture
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want a clear navigation structure organized by role and depth so that I can find exactly what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/config/navigation.ts has new topNavItems: Docs, SDK, Blog (placeholder), GitHub
sideNavItems has 5 sections: Getting Started, Guides, SDK Reference, Server Reference, Contributing
Getting Started: Quick Start, Installation, Your First Plugin
Guides: Plugin Development, Error Handling, Logging & Debugging, Resources & Prompts, Streaming & Progress
SDK Reference: Plugin Class, Tools, Resources, Prompts, Utilities (DOM, Fetch, Storage, Timing), Lifecycle Hooks, Error Types
Server Reference: CLI Commands, Configuration, MCP Server, Browser Tools, Troubleshooting
Contributing: Development Setup, Architecture, Publishing
Tab State Machine page is removed from sidebar (internal implementation detail, not user-facing value)
Plugin System concepts page is merged into the Plugin Development guide
Architecture page moves to Contributing section (deep technical content for contributors)
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/config/navigation.ts. The new structure follows progressive depth: Getting Started (anyone), Guides (plugin developers), SDK Reference (plugin developers looking up API), Server Reference (users and developers), Contributing (platform contributors). The topNavItems should be: [{ title: 'Docs', href: '/docs' }, { title: 'SDK', href: '/docs/sdk/plugin-class' }, { title: 'GitHub', href: 'https://github.com/AnomalyCo/opentabs', external: true }]. Remove the 'Plugins' link that points to a single plugin page (Slack). The SDK shortcut gives quick access to the most-referenced section. Consider adding a 'Changelog' link later. NOTE: Creating new pages is done in later stories — this story only updates the navigation config. Pages that don't exist yet should point to their future paths. The build may fail if pages are missing — that's OK, later stories create them.

userStories.title: Write Quick Start page — zero to working in 5 minutes
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a 5-minute quick start that gets me from nothing to a working tool call so that I can see value immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx exists
Page covers in order: install CLI, run opentabs start, load extension in Chrome, configure MCP client, install a plugin, make a tool call
Each step is numbered with a shell command and expected output
Total reading time under 3 minutes
No concepts explained — just do this, see that
Links to Installation for detailed setup, Plugin Development for building your own
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx. Frontmatter: title 'Quick Start', description '5 minutes from install to your first tool call.' This should be the FIRST page new users see. Keep it extremely concise — 6 steps maximum. Use code blocks for every command. Show the expected opentabs start output. Show the MCP client config JSON. Show an example tool call. Reference opentabs plugin search for finding plugins. Do NOT explain architecture, concepts, or how things work — just show the steps.

userStories.title: Rewrite Installation page with role-based sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want installation instructions tailored to my role so that I install only what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx is rewritten
Has three clear sections: For Users (npm install CLI + opentabs start), For Plugin Developers (+ bun, + scaffolding), For Contributors (+ clone repo, + bun run dev)
Each section is self-contained — a reader can follow just their section
Prerequisites are listed clearly (Node.js 18+ for users, Bun for developers)
Uses the CliCommand component for multi-package-manager install tabs where appropriate
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Rewrite docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx. The current page assumes everyone is a platform developer (clone, build, load extension). The rewrite should start with the simplest path (user: npm install -g, opentabs start) and progressively add complexity. Users don't need Bun. Plugin developers need Bun for their plugin projects. Contributors need the full monorepo setup.

userStories.title: Write Plugin Development guide — the complete walkthrough
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want a comprehensive guide that walks me through creating, building, testing, and publishing a plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx exists
Covers: scaffold a plugin, understand the project structure, define tools, implement isReady(), use SDK utilities, build and test, hot reload workflow, publish to npm
Each section has working code examples
References SDK Reference pages for detailed API docs
Replaces the old creating-a-plugin.mdx (which can be deleted or redirected)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx. This is the flagship guide — it should be the best plugin development guide in any MCP ecosystem. Start with opentabs plugin create, show the generated file structure, explain each file, then build up a real tool step by step. Show how opentabs-plugin build auto-registers in config and calls /reload. Show the bun run dev workflow. Show how to use sdk.fetchJSON, sdk.waitForSelector, sdk.retry. End with npm publish and how users install it. Tone: friendly, encouraging, like a friend showing you around.

userStories.title: Write Error Handling guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to understand how to use structured errors so that AI agents can make intelligent retry decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx exists
Covers: ToolError class, error categories, retryable errors, error factories, how errors propagate to AI agents
Shows code examples for each error factory: ToolError.auth(), .notFound(), .rateLimited(), .timeout(), .validation(), .internal()
Explains what AI agents see and how they use the structured fields
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx. Keep it practical — show code first, explain second. The key message: structured errors help AI agents retry intelligently. A ToolError.rateLimited('Too many requests', 2000) tells Claude to wait 2 seconds and try again, instead of giving up or guessing.

userStories.title: Write Logging & Debugging guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to understand how to debug my plugin so that I can fix issues efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx exists
Covers: sdk.log API, where logs appear (server log, MCP client, opentabs logs), opentabs doctor, opentabs status, opentabs audit, Chrome DevTools for extension debugging
Shows a debugging workflow: something fails → check opentabs status → check opentabs logs → check opentabs audit → use sdk.log in handler
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx. This is the 'when things go wrong' page. Walk through a realistic debugging scenario. Show sdk.log.info('Fetching channels...') in a tool handler. Show where that log ends up. Show opentabs audit output. Show opentabs doctor output.

userStories.title: Write Resources & Prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to understand how to define resources and prompts so that AI agents can access contextual data.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx exists
Covers: what resources are (read-only data), what prompts are (pre-built templates), defineResource API, definePrompt API, dispatch flow
Shows practical examples: a resource that lists items, a prompt that generates a summary
Explains when to use tools vs resources vs prompts
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx. The key distinction to explain: tools are actions (send a message), resources are data (list of channels), prompts are templates (summarize this thread). Most plugins will primarily use tools, but resources are powerful for giving AI agents browsable context.

userStories.title: Write Streaming & Progress guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to understand how to report progress for long-running operations.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx exists
Covers: reportProgress callback, how progress flows to the AI agent, timeout behavior with progress, when to use it
Shows a practical example: a search tool that reports progress as it processes results
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx. Keep it short — this is a focused feature guide. Show the defineTool handle signature with context param, show reportProgress call, explain that it resets the timeout.

userStories.title: Write SDK Reference pages — Plugin Class, Tools, Resources, Prompts
userStories.description: As a plugin developer looking up API details, I want precise reference documentation for the core SDK types.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx — OpenTabsPlugin base class, all properties, isReady, teardown, lifecycle hooks
docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx — defineTool factory, handle signature, context param, input/output schemas
docs/content/docs/sdk/resources.mdx — defineResource factory, read handler, URI patterns
docs/content/docs/sdk/prompts.mdx — definePrompt factory, render handler, arguments
Each page has: type signatures, parameter tables, return types, code examples, notes/gotchas
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create 4 files in docs/content/docs/sdk/. These are API reference pages — precise, complete, not tutorial-style. Each should have the full TypeScript type signature, a parameters table, a return type description, at least one code example, and any important notes. Link back to the Plugin Development guide for walkthrough context.

userStories.title: Write SDK Reference pages — Utilities (DOM, Fetch, Storage, Timing), Lifecycle Hooks, Error Types
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want reference docs for all SDK utilities and types.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx — all SDK utilities organized by category (DOM, Fetch, Storage, Page State, Timing), each with signature, description, example
docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx — all hooks (onNavigate, onActivate, onDeactivate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd) with when they fire, arguments, examples
docs/content/docs/sdk/error-types.mdx — ToolError class, constructor, properties, factory methods, error categories, propagation behavior
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create 3 files in docs/content/docs/sdk/. The utilities page should be comprehensive — every function listed with its signature, a one-line description, and a code example. Group by category with clear headings. The lifecycle hooks page should have a diagram or table showing the lifecycle timeline. The error types page should list all factory methods with examples.

userStories.title: Rewrite Server Reference pages — CLI, Configuration, MCP Server, Browser Tools, Troubleshooting
userStories.description: As a user or developer, I want accurate reference docs for the CLI, config, server, and troubleshooting.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx is rewritten for the new CLI structure (opentabs start, status, doctor, logs, audit, plugin create, plugin search, config show/set/path)
docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx is rewritten for the new config format (localPlugins, tools, secret — no more plugins array or npmPlugins)
docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx is updated for new endpoints (/audit, ungated /reload) and capabilities (resources, prompts)
docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx is kept as-is (already comprehensive) or lightly updated
docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx is updated for new workflows (opentabs start auto-init, auto-discover, etc.)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Rewrite/update 5 files in docs/content/docs/reference/. The CLI page needs the most work — remove dev, build, reload, setup, config init, plugin add/remove/list commands. Add start, audit, plugin search. The config page needs to show localPlugins instead of plugins, remove npmPlugins. The MCP server page needs /audit endpoint and resources/prompts capabilities. Troubleshooting should reference opentabs doctor and opentabs logs as first steps.

userStories.title: Write Contributing section — Development Setup, Architecture, Publishing
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want guides for setting up the dev environment, understanding the architecture, and publishing packages.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx — clone, bun install, bun run dev, how the dev orchestrator works, how to run tests
docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx — moved from concepts/architecture.mdx, updated for new discovery, registry, config, dev/prod modes
docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx — how to publish packages, dependency order, single npm token, updating plugins after publish
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create 3 files in docs/content/docs/contributing/. The dev-setup page should be a step-by-step guide to getting the full platform running locally. The architecture page is the current concepts/architecture.mdx moved and updated — it's excellent content that belongs in the contributor section, not in the user-facing concepts section. The publishing page documents the publish.sh script and the npm token setup.

userStories.title: Rewrite Introduction page
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want a clear introduction that tells me what OpenTabs is, who it's for, and where to go next.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/index.mdx is rewritten
Opens with a one-sentence description of what OpenTabs does
Has a 'Who is this for?' section with three roles (users, plugin developers, contributors) and links to the right starting page for each
Has a concise 'How it works' section (3 steps, not a table)
Has a 'Key features' section with the most important 4-5 features
Ends with clear next steps linking to Quick Start
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Rewrite docs/content/docs/index.mdx. The current page is good but starts with a table (dry). The rewrite should open with warmth: 'OpenTabs gives AI agents access to any web application through your browser.' Then immediately offer role-based paths: 'Want to use it? → Quick Start. Want to build a plugin? → Plugin Development. Want to contribute? → Development Setup.' The how-it-works section should be a simple numbered list, not a table.

userStories.title: Delete outdated pages and create redirects
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want to remove outdated content so that readers don't encounter stale information.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/concepts/tab-state-machine.mdx is deleted (internal implementation detail)
docs/content/docs/concepts/plugin-system.mdx is deleted (merged into Plugin Development guide)
docs/content/docs/plugins/creating-a-plugin.mdx is deleted (replaced by Plugin Development guide)
docs/content/docs/plugins/plugin-sdk.mdx is deleted (replaced by SDK Reference pages)
docs/content/docs/plugins/slack.mdx is moved to docs/content/docs/reference/slack-plugin.mdx or kept as a reference
No broken internal links remain in any MDX file
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Delete the outdated files. Search all remaining MDX files for links to deleted pages and update them to point to the new locations. The Slack plugin page is useful as a reference — keep it but move it under reference/ or a new 'examples' section. Run the build to verify no broken links.

userStories.title: Create SVG illustrations for key pages
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want visual illustrations that help me understand complex concepts at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Quick Start page has a simple 3-step flow illustration (Install → Start → Use) matching RetroUI style
Plugin Development guide has a plugin project structure illustration
At least 2 illustrations are inline SVGs using CSS variables for theme-awareness (like the homepage ArchitectureIllustration)
Illustrations use the neo-brutalist style: hard shadows, thick borders, monospace text, primary color accents, no border-radius
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create inline SVG components in the MDX files or as React components in docs/components/. Follow the exact style of the ArchitectureIllustration in docs/app/(marketing)/page.tsx: use CSS variables (var(--color-foreground), var(--color-primary), var(--color-background)), var(--font-mono) for text, 3px strokeWidth, hard drop shadows (offset rect), and the box-with-header-bar pattern. Keep illustrations simple and focused — they should clarify, not decorate.

userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the header bar and homepage to reflect the new information architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: TopNav.tsx header has links: Docs, SDK, GitHub (with external link icon), dark mode toggle
Docs links to /docs (introduction)
SDK links to /docs/sdk/plugin-class (first SDK reference page)
GitHub opens in new tab
Homepage (marketing page) CTA buttons link to Quick Start instead of Installation
Homepage 'How it works' section is accurate for the new workflows
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/components/TopNav.tsx and docs/config/navigation.ts topNavItems. Edit docs/app/(marketing)/page.tsx to update CTA links. The current Plugins link in the header goes to a single plugin page (Slack) which is not useful. Replace with SDK which is the most-referenced section for developers.

userStories.title: Final verification — all pages render, no broken links, build passes
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want all docs pages to build and render correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: cd docs && bun run build passes with zero errors
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
All internal links in MDX files resolve to existing pages
No references to opentabs-plugin.json, npmPlugins, opentabs build, opentabs dev, opentabs reload, opentabs setup, config init, plugin add, plugin remove, plugin list in any MDX file
Every page in the sidebar navigation renders correctly
